person.name: Matilda "Maud" de Emildon
person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
«b»Biography«/b»
Maud (1310 - 1369), is the daughter of Richard Emeldon (d. 1333).

«b»Marriage and Issue«/b»
After her first husband Richard de Acton died in 1342, "Maud" became sole heir.

She outlived both husbands, her daughter, and her brother-in-law Nicholas de Acton and his barren wife Alice.

The family estates then passed to Maud and Richard's grandchildren through their daughter Elizabeth. She was an only child.

m.1 (ante 1333) Richard de Acton (d.1342). 

«b»Issue:«/b»
1.) Elizabeth de Acton m. Roger Widdrington

m.2 (by 1351) Alexander Hilton (d.1361). No issue.

sourceList.title: The Local Historian's Table Book, of Remarkable Occurences, Historical Facts, Traditions, Legendary and Descriptive Ballads, &c., &c, Volume 2

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Emildon
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Devon): habitational name from Mildon in Oakford (Devon), which was named in Old English with middel ‘middle’ + dūn ‘hill’. Alternatively, it may be from Meldon in Okehampton, which is probably named with Old English mǣle ‘multi-colored, variegated’ + dūn ‘hill’.

nameMeanings.name: Matilda
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Latinized form of a Germanic personal name derived from maht, meht ‘might’ + hild ‘battle’. This was the name of an early German queen ( 895–968 ), wife of Henry the Fowler, who was noted for her piety and generosity. It was also the name of the wife of William the Conqueror and of the daughter of Henry I of England ( see Maud ). The name was introduced into England by the Normans, and this Latinized form is the one that normally occurs in medieval records, while the vernacular form Maud was the one in everyday use. Matilda was revived in England as a learned form in the 18th century.

variants.name: Mathilda
meanings: Latinized form of a Germanic personal name derived from maht, meht ‘might’ + hild ‘battle’. This was the name of an early German queen ( 895–968 ), wife of Henry the Fowler, who was noted for her piety and generosity. It was also the name of the wife of William the Conqueror and of the daughter of Henry I of England ( see Maud ). The name was introduced into England by the Normans, and this Latinized form is the one that normally occurs in medieval records, while the vernacular form Maud was the one in everyday use. Matilda was revived in England as a learned form in the 18th century.

nameMeanings.name: "Maud"
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Medieval vernacular form of Matilda . This form was characteristically Low German (i.e. including medieval Dutch and Flemish). The wife of William the Conqueror , who bore this name, was the daughter of Baldwin , Count of Flanders. In Flemish and Dutch the letter -t- was generally lost when it occurred between vowels, giving forms such as Ma(h)auld. Maud or Matilda was also the name of the daughter ( 1102–67 ) of Henry I of England; she was married early in life to the Holy Roman Emperor Henry V , and later disputed the throne of England with her cousin Stephen . In 1128 she married Geoffrey , Count of Anjou. A medieval chronicler commented, ‘she was a good woman, but she had little bliss with him’. The name Maud became quite common in England in the 19th century, when its popularity was influenced in part by Tennyson's poem Maud, published in 1855 , but has not been much used since the early decades of the 20th century.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Matilda "Maud" de Emildon was born in 1310, in Newcastle upon Tyne, Northumberland, England, her father, Richard Emildon, was 35 and her mother, Christina De Mowbray, was 25. She had at least 1 daughter with Richard Acton. She died on 8 September 1369, in her hometown, at the age of 59.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Matilda "Maud" d’Emildon est née en 1310, à Newcastle upon Tyne, Northumberland, Angleterre, son père, Richard Emildon, avait 35 ans et sa mère, Christina De Mowbray, avait 25 ans. Elle a eu au moins 1 fille avec Richard Acton. Elle est décédée le 8 septembre 1369, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 59 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Matilda "Maud" de Emildon 1310 in Newcastle upon Tyne, Northumberland, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Richard Emildon 35 und ihre Mutter Christina De Mowbray 25. Sie hatte mit Richard Acton mindestens 1 Tochter. Sie starb am 8. September 1369 im Alter von 59 Jahren in ihrem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Matilda "Maud" de Emildon è nata il nel 1310 nel Newcastle upon Tyne, Northumberland, England, sua padre, Richard Emildon, aveva 35 anni e sua madre, Christina De Mowbray, aveva 25 anni. Lei avuto almeno 1 figlia con Richard Acton. Morì il 8 settembre 1369, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 59 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Matilda "Maud" de Emildon(이)가 1310년 영국 노르스벌랜드 뉴카스틀 업 타이니에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Richard Emildon(은)는 35세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Christina De Mowbray(은)는 25세였다. 그녀는 Richard Acton(와)과 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1369년 9월 8일 그녀의 고향에서 59세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Matilda "Maud" de Emildon родилась в 1310 г. в местности нюкастл упон тин, нортумбирланд, Англия, её отцу по имени Richard Emildon было 35 лет, а матери, которую звали Christina De Mowbray, 25. es.lifeSummary: Cuando Matilda "Maud" de Emildon nació en 1310, en Newcastle upon Tyne, Northumberland, Inglaterra, su papá, Richard Emildon, tenía 35 años y su mamá, Christina De Mowbray, tenía 25 años. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hija con Richard Acton. Murió el 8 de septiembre de 1369, en su pueblo, a la edad de 59 años.
